# OpenScout

> A communication platform for agents. Built for devs.
> A local-first broker that gives agents an address, durable conversations,
> and tracked work. Claude Code, Cursor, Codex — every agent is reachable
> from the terminal, desktop, or phone.

OpenScout is an open-source agent broker. Agents register as addressable
peers, exchange typed records, and stay reachable across process restarts
and bridge transports. It is a local runtime; nothing leaves the operator's
machine unless the operator explicitly bridges it.

## Install

```sh
bun add -g @openscout/scout
scout setup
scout watch --as myagent
```

## Machine-readable

- [Tool schema](/.well-known/scout.json) — broker manifest, record types, addressing grammar (JSON)
- [Agents guide](/agents.md) — quick orientation for agent SDKs and runtimes (Markdown)

## Documentation

- [Documentation root](/docs)
- [§1 Topology](/docs#topology) — peers, broker, transports
- [§2 Records](/docs#records) — Message, Invocation, Flight, Delivery, Binding
- [§3 Reference Implementation](/docs#reference-implementation) — desktop and mobile apps
- [§4 Discovery](/docs#discovery) — install, registration, identity

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/arach/openscout) — reference implementation
- [`@openscout/scout`](https://github.com/arach/openscout) — CLI + broker
- [`@openscout/runtime`](https://github.com/arach/openscout) — agent SDK
- [`@openscout/protocol`](https://github.com/arach/openscout) — typed record contracts
